title: Integrate CNI firewall plugin code
body: Replace our old manual iptables hangling with (modified) vendored code from https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins/pull/75. This should greatly improve our interaction with the system firewall.

comments.body:
@dcbw PTAL - had to modify your code a bit for rootless support and non-Linux builds, but it's basically identical if a bit rearranged.

@baude @rhatdan PTAL
bot, retest this please
bot, retest this please

Something strange is happening in the test suites for a while, where /dev/shm seems to be switching?

```
/go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/run_ns_test.go:11
  podman run ipcns test [It]
  /go/src/github.com/containers/libpod/test/e2e/run_ns_test.go:50

  Expected
      <string>: 294298 /dev/shm
  to equal
      <string>: 158124 /dev/shm
```
bot, retest this please
/cc @miabbott @mike-nguyen
An update here: this does not appear to resolve our port forwarding issues. Investigating now.

Alright, documenting my findings here before I'm off for lunch:
- Remote port forwarding always works, but you may need a system restart to clear the firewall and make it start working
- Local port forwarding works until anything else starts messing with iptables. And I mean anything. Even listing rules via `iptables -nvL` will cause local forwarding to break.

Further details:
After an `iptables -nvL` or `iptables-save`, packets from localhost (to either 127.0.0.1 or any external IP) successfully make it to the CNI-HOSTPORT-DNAT prerouting chain, but either are not forwarded to or are not matched by the per-container CNI-DN-<ID> chain.
bot, retest this please

LGTM as is ... I would like to see this merged.  Functionally it is doing what we expected even though more debug on other uses cases and environments is needed.  Merging will also allow us and others to use a COPR package for testing.
I think we're in good shape, the other issues seem unrelated.

:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.

Can someone give me an info if this is fixed in 1.4.4 version of podman?
I am facing this issue on my RHEL 7.7 machine at my customers on-prem systems.
Definitely should be. This was resolved before we went 1.0.

I can say that on a fresh RHEL 7.7 system with latest Podman version provided by RedHat I can reproduce this issue.

Steps to reproduce:

- Download latest RHEL 7.7 ISO
- Install as standard server, with or without GUI doesnt matter
- Install Podman
- Run following commands
- Between the both `curl`'s run `firewall-cmd --reload` on another terminal

```
[root@rhel-local sergeiwaigant]# cat /etc/redhat-release
Red Hat Enterprise Linux Server release 7.7 (Maipo)
[root@rhel-local sergeiwaigant]# uname -mrs
Linux 3.10.0-1062.el7.x86_64 x86_64
[root@rhel-local sergeiwaigant]# podman --version
podman version 1.4.4

[root@rhel-local sergeiwaigant]# podman run --rm -ti registry.access.redhat.com/rhel7
[root@b860133fee69 /]# curl ifconfig.co
4x.1xx.2xx.1xx
[root@b860133fee69 /]# curl ifconfig.co
curl: (6) Could not resolve host: ifconfig.co; Unknown error
root@b860133fee69 /]# exit
ERRO[0157] Error deleting network: running [/sbin/iptables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s 10.88.0.24 -j CNI-f50e48a52fa26aaaad634e9a -m comment --comment name: "podman" id: "b860133fee6983ea2b8bab495e1beffac0f1fd2232db8d923d57c12d67b08649" --wait]: exit status 2: iptables v1.4.21: Couldn't load target `CNI-f50e48a52fa26aaaad634e9a':No such file or directory

Try `iptables -h' or 'iptables --help' for more information.
ERRO[0157] Error while removing pod from CNI network "podman": running [/sbin/iptables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s 10.88.0.24 -j CNI-f50e48a52fa26aaaad634e9a -m comment --comment name: "podman" id: "b860133fee6983ea2b8bab495e1beffac0f1fd2232db8d923d57c12d67b08649" --wait]: exit status 2: iptables v1.4.21: Couldn't load target `CNI-f50e48a52fa26aaaad634e9a':No such file or directory

Try `iptables -h' or 'iptables --help' for more information.
ERRO[0157] unable to cleanup network for container b860133fee6983ea2b8bab495e1beffac0f1fd2232db8d923d57c12d67b08649: "error tearing down CNI namespace configuration for container b860133fee6983ea2b8bab495e1beffac0f1fd2232db8d923d57c12d67b08649: running [/sbin/iptables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s 10.88.0.24 -j CNI-f50e48a52fa26aaaad634e9a -m comment --comment name: \"podman\" id: \"b860133fee6983ea2b8bab495e1beffac0f1fd2232db8d923d57c12d67b08649\" --wait]: exit status 2: iptables v1.4.21: Couldn't load target `CNI-f50e48a52fa26aaaad634e9a':No such file or directory\n\nTry `iptables -h' or 'iptables --help' for more information.\n"
```

Regards
-Sergei

Firewall reload wiping our rules is a known issue, with the recommended solution being to restart the containers. I recommend opening a bugzilla if this is considered a critical or blocking issue so we can prioritize appropriately.

Hm its kind of blocking at my client, with a workaround available, though... just fall back to Docker as container runtime for time being. Unless they stick to 7.7 and not go to 8 the Docker package is available.
Then it becomes blocking and crticial without a workaround :D

Edit: Unfortunately the client has some Puppet modules which are reloading firewalld from time to time scheduled from a central point in the network... We must deal with this situation since this modules are security related implementations.

Is this then now more firewall related or podman / CNI related? Didnt get the underlying problem exactly.
Is there a an open issue on GitHub? couldnt find it...

I will file a bugzilla for this, pointing to the problem.
@sergeiwaigant did you file a bz? I could not locate one
@Klaas- nope, not so far... will retry this on a fresh system here once and then file a bz.
The same behaviour explained by @sergeiwaigant is happening with RH8.1
